description: TraceQL feature showcase: one panel target per traceql-feature-inventory.json row, enforced by test/inventory. Panels carry cerberus.expect contracts where the defined outcome is empty or an error.

panels.description: Every attribute-carrier scope the OTel-CH schema backs: span.* (SpanAttributes), resource.* (ResourceAttributes), bare .attr (span scope by TraceQL default).
panels.title: Span / resource / unscoped attribute scopes

panels.description: event.* / link.* predicates lower to arrayExists over the Events/Links Nested Attributes maps; the regex form pins the match() emit fix (raw '=~' was a ClickHouse syntax error).
panels.title: Event & link scoped attributes

panels.description: The five core span intrinsics, each resolving to its dedicated OTel-CH column (SpanName / Duration / StatusCode / SpanKind / StatusMessage).
panels.title: name / duration / status / kind / statusMessage

panels.description: Identity intrinsics over TraceId / SpanId / ParentSpanId. The parent intrinsic is the direct parent-span-id equality form.
panels.title: trace:id / span:id / parent

panels.description: Nested intrinsics target the Events/Links Nested subfields (Events.Name, Links.TraceId, Links.SpanId); instrumentation:name/version map to ScopeName/ScopeVersion.
panels.title: event:name / link IDs / instrumentation scope

panels.description: The ordered + inequality + regex comparison family over intrinsics.
panels.title: Comparison operators

panels.description: `!= nil` lowers to mapContains on the scope carrier (the Traces Drilldown app stamps this on every breakdown view); `= nil` is the negation — every span matches an attribute nobody ever writes.
panels.title: nil checks (existence probes)

panels.description: Field-expression conjunction and disjunction over DIFFERENT attributes (same-attribute || is the parser's IN rewrite — see the parity-rejections row).
panels.title: Boolean logic && / ||

panels.description: All six arithmetic operators against the seeded payload_bytes span attribute; toFloat64OrNull coercion keeps spans without the attribute out of the result instead of aborting the query.
panels.title: Arithmetic on numeric attributes

panels.description: Numeric coercion (toFloat64OrNull) for int/float literals; bool literals coerce to the OTel-CH 'true'/'false' string encoding. String/duration/status/kind literals are exercised by the intrinsic panels above.
panels.title: Typed literals: int / float / bool

panels.description: gateway > shop selects shop spans directly under a gateway span; payments < shop selects the parent side of the payments→shop edge.
panels.title: Direct child / parent ( > , < )

panels.description: WITH RECURSIVE closure over ParentSpanId: db spans live two hops below the gateway root in the seeded checkout trace.
panels.title: Recursive descendant / ancestor ( >> , << )

panels.description: charge (payments) and orders.insert (db) share the shop checkout span as parent.
panels.title: Sibling ( ~ )

panels.description: LEFT ANTI JOIN forms: queue spans never relate to payments spans in any seeded trace, so every negated relation returns the full RHS spanset.
panels.title: Negated structural ( !> !< !>> !<< !~ )

panels.description: UNION DISTINCT forms returning both sides of each matched relation.
panels.title: Union structural ( &> &< &>> &<< &~ )

panels.description: Set intersection (INNER JOIN on TraceId+SpanId) and union (UNION DISTINCT).
panels.title: Spanset && / ||

panels.description: count/sum/avg/min/max per trace with the HAVING-style scalar filter — the only grammatical form spanset aggregates appear in.
panels.title: Scalar-filter aggregates

panels.description: Projection, grouping (one representative span per group) and spanset dedup over a set-op result.
panels.title: select / by / coalesce

panels.description: Root-ness is the one nested-set question the OTel-CH schema can answer exactly: nestedSetParent < 0 lowers to ParentSpanId = '' (what Grafana's Traces Drilldown stamps on every query); >= 0 is the non-root complement.
panels.title: nestedSetParent root idiom (Traces Drilldown)

cerberus.why: The needle name is never seeded and never emitted by self-telemetry; the CORRECT result of this search is zero traces. Bidirectional: rows appearing here would mean the filter stopped filtering.

panels.description: No span ever carries this name; the panel proves the empty-result path renders cleanly and the bidirectional pin fails the sweep if it ever returns rows.
panels.title: Defined-empty: impossible needle

panels.description: The exact query Grafana Traces Drilldown's Structure tab sends (StructureScene.tsx buildQuery, rate metric): a union-structural arm OR'd with the root-span primary signal, piped into select() with the three nested-set intrinsics. Cerberus recomputes Tempo's ingest-time nested-set numbering at query time (NestedSetAnnotate) and surfaces the values in spanSets[].spans[].attributes.

targets.query: ({ nestedSetParent < 0 } &>> { kind = server }) || ({ nestedSetParent < 0 }) | select(status, resource.service.name, name, nestedSetParent, nestedSetLeft, nestedSetRight)

panels.title: select(nestedSet*) — Drilldown structure tab

panels.description: Span-rate over the matrix anchor grid; the by() form partitions per resource service.
panels.title: rate() — bare and by()

panels.description: Per-step span counts, bare and grouped by span name.
panels.title: count_over_time()

panels.description: Duration-operand reducers; values rebase from Int64 ns to seconds to match Tempo's wire shape.
panels.title: min / max / avg over time (duration)

panels.description: Numeric span-attribute operand (payload_bytes) with toFloat64OrZero aggregate coercion.
panels.title: sum_over_time over a numeric attribute

panels.description: Three phi values fan out one series each (p label), folded from power-of-two bucket rows via Tempo's Log2QuantileWithBucket.
panels.title: quantile_over_time multi-phi

panels.description: Per-bucket distribution series — one series per power-of-two bucket edge.
panels.title: histogram_over_time

panels.description: The with() hint block parses and rides along; exemplars are attached to the matrix response.
panels.title: Hints: with(exemplars = true)

panels.description: Per-anchor series selection (LIMIT K BY anchor_ts) over the rate-by-name matrix.
panels.title: Second stage: topk / bottomk

panels.description: The metrics filter form (no pipe before the comparison): keeps only data points whose Value clears the threshold.
panels.title: Second stage: threshold filter

cerberus.expect: error:depends on nested-set positions the OTel ClickHouse schema does not materialise
cerberus.why: Position-dependent nested-set comparisons need the materialised nested-set tree Tempo builds at ingest; OTel-CH has no equivalent columns. Only root-ness shapes lower (see the root-idiom panel).

panels.description: Cerberus rejects `{ nestedSetParent = 5 }` today; this panel pins the rejection bidirectionally.
panels.title: nestedSetParent position — parity rejection

cerberus.why: Left/right nested-set bounds have no OTel-CH backing column; cerberus rejects rather than fabricating positions.

panels.description: Cerberus rejects `{ nestedSetLeft > 0 }` today; this panel pins the rejection bidirectionally.
panels.title: nestedSetLeft — parity rejection

cerberus.why: Left/right nested-set bounds have no OTel-CH backing column; cerberus rejects rather than fabricating positions.

panels.description: Cerberus rejects `{ nestedSetRight > 0 }` today; this panel pins the rejection bidirectionally.
panels.title: nestedSetRight — parity rejection

cerberus.why: rootName needs whole-trace evaluation (find the root span per trace); the OTel-CH span-row schema stores one row per span. Previously this lowered to a SpanAttributes['rootName'] lookup that silently matched nothing — the rejection pins the honest boundary.

panels.description: Cerberus rejects `{ rootName = "GET /api/checkout" }` today; this panel pins the rejection bidirectionally.
panels.title: rootName — parity rejection

panels.description: Cerberus rejects `{ rootServiceName = "gateway" }` today; this panel pins the rejection bidirectionally.
panels.title: rootServiceName — parity rejection

cerberus.why: Whole-trace duration requires aggregating every span of the trace; the span-row schema cannot answer it per row.

panels.description: Cerberus rejects `{ traceDuration > 100ms }` today; this panel pins the rejection bidirectionally.
panels.title: traceDuration — parity rejection

cerberus.why: Child counts are a Tempo ingest-time materialisation; OTel-CH has no equivalent column.

panels.description: Cerberus rejects `{ span:childCount > 0 }` today; this panel pins the rejection bidirectionally.
panels.title: span:childCount — parity rejection

cerberus.why: Needs per-event Timestamp minus span start across the Events Nested column — unsupported; rejected loudly instead of misreading SpanAttributes.

panels.description: Cerberus rejects `{ event:timeSinceStart > 1ms }` today; this panel pins the rejection bidirectionally.
panels.title: event:timeSinceStart — parity rejection

cerberus.why: The upstream OTel-CH traces schema materialises ScopeName/ScopeVersion but no scope-attributes map; without a configured column the lookup would silently read SpanAttributes.

panels.description: Cerberus rejects `{ instrumentation.deployment = "blue" }` today; this panel pins the rejection bidirectionally.
panels.title: instrumentation.* scope attributes — parity rejection

cerberus.why: Tempo's parser optimiser rewrites same-attribute disjunctions into the internal IN operator, which cerberus does not lower yet; the rejection documents the boundary until an In-frag lowering lands.

panels.description: Cerberus rejects `{ name = "GET /api/checkout" || name = "POST /api/orders" }` today; this panel pins the rejection bidirectionally.
panels.title: same-attribute || (IN rewrite) — parity rejection

cerberus.why: Field-expression negation is not lowered yet; the rejection documents the boundary.

panels.description: Cerberus rejects `{ !(kind = server) }` today; this panel pins the rejection bidirectionally.
panels.title: unary ! negation — parity rejection

cerberus.why: compare() lowers through chplan.MetricsCompare since feat/traceql-compare: the SQL explodes every span attribute into (cohort, attr, value) counts and the handler mirrors Tempo's BaselineAggregator (__meta_type series scheme). The seed always carries status=error spans, so the panel must render series — an empty result or a 422 regression re-fires this pin.

panels.description: `{ } | compare({ status = error })` — baseline-vs-selection attribute split (Grafana Traces Drilldown's Comparison tab shape). Formerly a pinned 422 rejection; the contract flipped to nonempty when compare() support landed.
panels.title: compare() — baseline vs selection

title: TraceQL Showcase
